title: Validate API Input with DTOs and Pipes
prompt: Use $nestjs-best-practices to improve this NestJS code according to api-validation-dtos.
notes:
# Validation DTO Dataset

Use this case when raw payloads or `any` leak into services. The app should also enable `ValidationPipe` globally with whitelist behavior in bootstrap.

title: Keep Features in Cohesive NestJS Modules
prompt: Use $nestjs-best-practices to improve this NestJS code according to arch-feature-modules.
notes:
# Feature Module Dataset

Use this case when AppModule is accumulating feature internals. The correction creates a feature boundary and exports only the service other modules are allowed to depend on.

title: Use Interceptors for Cross-Cutting Response Flow
prompt: Use $nestjs-best-practices to improve this NestJS code according to cross-interceptor-boundary.
notes:
# Interceptor Boundary Dataset

Use this case when every controller repeats response shaping, timing, or metadata. Prefer an interceptor for response-side cross-cutting behavior.

title: Reuse PrismaClient and Respect Connection Pools
prompt: Use $nestjs-best-practices to improve this NestJS code according to data-connection-pool-lifecycle.
notes:
# Connection Pool Dataset

Use this when PrismaClient is created per request or per method. NestJS apps should reuse one app-scoped provider.

title: Enforce Invariants with Database Constraints
prompt: Use $nestjs-best-practices to improve this NestJS code according to data-constraint-integrity.
notes:
# Constraint Integrity Dataset

Use this when a NestJS service enforces uniqueness, ownership, or relationships only in application code. Move durable invariants into the database schema and map constraint errors to domain/API errors.

title: Keep Transactions Short and Lock Order Consistent
prompt: Use $nestjs-best-practices to improve this NestJS code according to data-long-transaction-locks.
notes:
# Long Transaction Dataset

Use this when external I/O or slow work happens inside `$transaction`. Keep DB transactions short and acquire locks consistently.

title: Separate Development Push from Production Migrations
prompt: Use $nestjs-best-practices to improve this NestJS code according to data-migration-safety.
notes:
# Migration Safety Dataset

Use this when app startup mutates schema. Production schema changes should be reviewed migrations run by deployment automation before the NestJS process starts.

title: Add Indexes for Hot Filters and Sorts
prompt: Use $nestjs-best-practices to improve this NestJS code according to data-missing-index.
notes:
# Missing Index Dataset

Use this when a growing table is filtered and sorted on an endpoint path. Confirm with query plans and match composite indexes to equality filters before range/sort columns.

title: Batch Relation Reads to Avoid N+1 Queries
prompt: Use $nestjs-best-practices to improve this NestJS code according to data-n-plus-one.
notes:
# N+1 Dataset

Use this when a resolver/service fetches children in a loop. Prefer relation loading or one batched query keyed by parent IDs.

title: Prefer Cursor Pagination for Large Lists
prompt: Use $nestjs-best-practices to improve this NestJS code according to data-offset-pagination.
notes:
# Offset Pagination Dataset

Use this when an API exposes deep page numbers on a large ordered list. Cursor pagination keeps pages stable and avoids expensive skipped rows.

title: Guard Read-Modify-Write Updates
prompt: Use $nestjs-best-practices to improve this NestJS code according to data-optimistic-concurrency.
notes:
# Optimistic Concurrency Dataset

Use this when service code reads a row, derives a value, then writes it back without checking whether another request changed the row. Prefer atomic increments or version predicates with retry behavior.

title: Select API Fields Intentionally
prompt: Use $nestjs-best-practices to improve this NestJS code according to data-overfetch-select.
notes:
# Overfetch Dataset

Use this when API code returns full records or broad relations. Narrow the database result to the public response contract.

title: Keep ORM Access Behind Feature Services
prompt: Use $nestjs-best-practices to improve this NestJS code according to data-prisma-service-boundary.
notes:
# Prisma Boundary Dataset

Use this case when controllers, guards, or resolvers call ORM clients directly. Move persistence detail to feature services so transactions and domain logic stay auditable.

title: Avoid Read-Then-Create Races
prompt: Use $nestjs-best-practices to improve this NestJS code according to data-race-condition-upsert.
notes:
# Race Condition Upsert Dataset

Use this when code performs a separate existence read before create/update. Require a database unique constraint and express the operation with `upsert` or explicit constraint-error handling.

title: Wrap Dependent Writes in One Transaction
prompt: Use $nestjs-best-practices to improve this NestJS code according to data-transaction-boundary.
notes:
# Transaction Boundary Dataset

Use this when multiple writes form one invariant. Put dependent writes in one transaction or nested write so partial failures cannot leak.

title: Bound Every List Query
prompt: Use $nestjs-best-practices to improve this NestJS code according to data-unbounded-result-set.
notes:
# Unbounded Result Set Dataset

Use this when API list handlers call `findMany` without `take`, cursor, or batching. Public endpoints should cap result sizes and expose a stable pagination contract.

title: Translate Domain Failures to NestJS Exceptions at Boundaries
prompt: Use $nestjs-best-practices to improve this NestJS code according to errors-domain-exceptions.
notes:
# Domain Exception Dataset

Use this case when controllers return ad hoc error objects. Throw NestJS exceptions and let filters or global exception handling produce the transport response.

title: Validate Configuration at Startup
prompt: Use $nestjs-best-practices to improve this NestJS code according to ops-config-validation.
notes:
# Config Validation Dataset

Use this case when providers read `process.env` directly. Centralize configuration and validate it during bootstrap so deployments fail fast.

title: Avoid Request-Scoped Providers Unless Required
prompt: Use $nestjs-best-practices to improve this NestJS code according to perf-request-scope.
notes:
# Request Scope Dataset

Use this case when request scope is applied to ordinary services. Request scope should be reserved for providers that truly need request-bound lifecycle or injected request data.

title: Put Auth and Authorization in Guards
prompt: Use $nestjs-best-practices to improve this NestJS code according to security-guard-composition.
notes:
# Guard Composition Dataset

Use this case when authorization policy is duplicated in controllers. Guards centralize policy and make route access visible through decorators.

title: Test NestJS Units with Provider Overrides
prompt: Use $nestjs-best-practices to improve this NestJS code according to test-provider-overrides.
notes:
# Provider Override Dataset

Use this case when unit tests import `AppModule` and accidentally boot databases, queues, or HTTP integrations. Override collaborators at the provider boundary.

title: Keep GraphQL Resolvers Thin
prompt: Use $nestjs-best-practices to improve this NestJS code according to transport-graphql-resolvers.
notes:
# GraphQL Resolver Dataset

Use this case when GraphQL resolvers include ORM or business logic. Keep resolvers as transport adapters and reuse feature services across REST, GraphQL, jobs, and tests.
